reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All gates passed. Spec violation confirmed in consensus/types/src/chain_spec.rs::min_epoch_data_availability_boundary::L828-830. When blob_retention_epoch > fulu_fork_epoch, line 829 returns current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS without enforcing max(result, FULU_FORK_EPOCH). During the window where current_epoch < FULU_FORK_EPOCH + 4096, the boundary can be set below FULU_FORK_EPOCH, violating the spec requirement from 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json invariant PROP-6a4369e9-inv-027: 'serve_range.start == max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH)'. An attacker can trigger this via P2P DataColumnsByRangeRequest during the 4096-epoch window after Fulu fork by requesting slots before FULU_FORK_EPOCH, causing nodes to accept requests for invalid epoch ranges (pre-fork data that should not exist), potentially causing DoS or inconsistent sync state. Function is actively called from data_availability_checker.rs and rpc_methods.rs (not dead code). Medium severity justified: affects individual nodes during a specific time window, does not reach >5% network threshold for High severity.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: data_column_serve_range lower bound must be max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH). Assertion: serve_range.start == max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH)
